---
title: "Pipeline overview"
execute:
  eval: false
---

PAGe produces prospective 1- and 2-week-ahead forecasts of respiratory-virus
percentage positivity. Within each surveillance week, its three stages run in
order because each stage supplies state to the next:

1. **M0 -- ignition detection** determines whether sustained seasonal activity
   has begun using only observations available through the current week.
2. **M1 -- alignment** aligns the partial season to historical templates and
   estimates epidemic phase, peak timing, and alignment uncertainty.
3. **M2 -- forecasting** combines real-time features with M1 covariates in a
   binomial GAM, then applies adaptive bias correction and an online seasonal
   effect.

## Data contract

The core input has one row per season and epidemiologic week. It includes
`season`, positive tests `y`, total tests `N`, positivity `p = y / N`, and a
within-season index `weekF`. PAGe keeps epidemiologic week, surveillance week,
flu week, ignition week, aligned week, and peak week distinct; these indices
must not be substituted for one another.

## Prospective evaluation

All tuning and validation use leave-one-season-out, walk-forward evaluation.
For a held-out season and issue week, model fitting and feature construction
may use historical seasons and observations available by that issue week, but
never later observations from the held-out season. M1 runs before M2 at every
issue week so that M2 receives only contemporaneously available alignment
features.

## Production configuration

The production M1 ensemble aligns on the logit scale and propagates
`logit_spread` to M2 as alignment uncertainty. The production M2 model is a
frozen GAM with level-only adaptive Holt EMA bias correction. The correction
rate is a deployment setting rather than a structural parameter in the nested
LOSO grid.

See the [pipeline walkthrough](pipeline-walkthrough.html) for the public API and
the [source map](source-map.html) for implementation ownership.
